dear customer , non-transferable cheques letter nov non-transferable cheques , would like address common enquiries received .
ordinary crossed cheques ordinary cheques crossings `` account payee '' , `` account payee '' , `` negotiable '' continue processed present automated clearing system without additional charges delay payment .
although cheques bearing `` account payee '' `` negotiable '' crossings remain transferable law , ordinary circumstances , offer adequate protection losses arising fraud misappropriation cheques banks ordinarily collect account payee cheques named payee .
, `` negotiable ' crossings cheques affected fraud , stolen , prevent fraudulent parties thieves subsequent transferee cheques acquiring good title .
' non-transferable cheques ?
non-transferable cheques include cheques crossing `` transferable '' bearing form crossing accompanied word `` '' payee 's name .
e.g .
pay `` xyz '' .
cheques subject charges take longer time cleared payment .
reasons manual processing non-transferable cheques unlike ordinary crossed cheques , non-transferable cheques impose additional responsibility paying bank ensure named payee receives funds .
however , paying bank ascertain automated clearing system allows paying bank make payment individual cheques collecting bank payees directly .
hence non-transferable cheques processed manually outside automated clearing system .
charges levied drawer ( & dollar ; ) payee ( & dollar ; ) necessary cover additional work required process cheques .
hope sufficiently clarified enquiries non-transferable cheques .
however , contact us need details .

curriculum broadly , curriculum could mean overall education programme institution embraces individual subject areas .
narrowly , equated syllabus subject contains set transmittable knowledge .
educationalists prefer confine curriculum overt activities planned organized intentionally administrators teachers students .
others speak curriculum include even things overtly included planning organizing learning arrangements learnt students .

used dyers different ethnicity , cultural background historical experience , found almost continents , though rarely primary method producing decorated fabrics .

clean space around paste-covered area background motif .

paste cleaned , motif shaped undyed negative space cloth .
second colour may added motif cloth dipped another dye bath , time , entire background space covered paste resist second dyeing .
batik dyed two three colours every colour takes one round resist-application dyeing .
process repeated many times colours required .

chapter nation-building singapore : historical perspective chiew seen kong singapore th century singapore small island malay archipelago .
situated southern tip peninsula west malaysia , linked short causeway .
stamford raffles landed island , malay chinese inhabitants .
colonized island established free port order promote trade .
soon chinese southern provinces china , indians , pakistanis ceylonese ( sri lankans ) indian subcontinent ceylon ( sri lanka ) , malays surrounding malay archipelago flocked population grew rapidly .
chinese population grown , outnumbered malay population , .
chinese recognizably became majority group singapore constituted % population .
mid- population .
million consists .

without police force army fearing ethnic conflicts , raffles segregated various ethnic groups geographically , assigning jetty singapore river areas hokkien chinese ( especially traders malacca ) british facilitate businesses .
policy led eventually formation ethnic sub-ethnic enclaves .
made mutual understanding impossible misunderstanding turn bred much prejudice stereotyping .
early settlers singapore , whether chinese , malay indian , predominantly illiterate semi-literate poor .
hand , british european traders colonial administrators wealthy powerful , doubt , likely looked successful models poor chinese , malays indians .
british aim single-mindedly make money exploiting every opportunity available singapore region .
chinese indian immigrants similarly motivated .
many opportunities create wealth new country created especially promote trade .
rapidly gave rise occupational trade specialization various ethnic sub-ethnic groups .
british european hokkien merchants dominated international trade , british indians prominent colonial administration public works , cantonese hakkas specialized building construction , hainanese food retail .
gradually disparity income wealth appeared widened among individuals ethnic groups trades occupations offered others .
thus slowly plural society evolved closely bonded ethnic groups divided culture , religion , language social class .
ethnic groups also segregated geographically socially differences language , religion trade pulled apart .
thus colony singapore culturally , demographically politically extension britain , malaya , indonesia , china india early settlers came make money returning homelands re-united families fellow countrymen .
social cohesion bind ethnic groups together .
comprise nation , unlikely nation-building instincts would arise competing disparate groups felt allegiance british trading colony .
long-term solutions plural societies today culturally homogeneous societies .
due conquest , colonization , international trade , forced migration ( slavery ) , voluntary migration , missionary activities forth , people diverse cultures , religions languages came co-exist within political boundaries .
thus plural societies common today .
plural societies divided four major categories : evolving due ( ) assimilation , ( ) integration , ( ) maintenance pluralism coercion , ( ) disintegration .
processes described briefly provide singaporeans broader perspective understanding singapore 's pattern ethnic relations likely direction change .
cultural assimilation : one-way process united states excellent example cultural assimilation reference white-black relations .
africans forcibly brought united states sold slaves two centuries ago .
african slaves came different parts africa .
spoke different languages , practised different customs religions .
working mainly plantations south , culturally heterogeneous .
coercion learned american english , dressed like american plantation workers , ate american food , eventually practised christianity .
two parallel processes transformed black peoples several generations black americans : gradual loss languages , religions customs , gradual assimilation culture white anglo-saxon protestant plantation masters whites .
thus one-way process .
slaves became culturally white today share little common africans .
due common experience slavery , oppression white masters , poverty liberation slavery developed black subculture still american african .
attempts return african roots failed .
cut long cultural roots - unable speak ancestral languages unfamiliar ancestral customs religions - revive basis totally absent .
, assimilation proceeded beyond certain point group lost touch culture , possible revive culture .
despite cultural assimilation , black americans discriminated white americans terms education , employment , voting rights , housing forth .
due sustained discrimination past , black americans generally lower educational attainment whites , lower income , poorer housing , still often accepted clubs donated whites .
short , cultural assimilation brought social assimilation , i.e. , equal status social acceptance dominant whites .
, however , many liberal whites ethnic groups sympathetic black americans ' cause desire full equality society , active supporters black protest movements .
cultural integration : processes fusion africans also sold portuguese plantations brazil sold american plantations .
initially portuguese captured amerindians forced work huge plantations .
amerindians came different tribes , spoke different languages practised different customs religious beliefs .
short , culturally heterogeneous .
hated sedentary work role slaves consequently low productivity .
portuguese turned africa source labour , bringing african slaves large numbers since average portuguese plantation brazil needed sixty workers .
american plantations small comparison .
portugal conquered moors northern africa .
moors kept slaves treated human beings rights .
portuguese thus learnt slavery moors followed example .
comparison , anglo-saxon american plantation owners treated slaves property property , slaves rights whatsoever .
brazil , portuguese sometimes sent skilled slaves ( e.g .
carpenters blacksmiths ) work cities .
slaves permitted keep earnings rest remitted masters .
thus could often accumulate enough buy back freedom .
reached old age , portuguese masters would free favourite slaves , act considered measure social grace brazilian society .
thus african slaves treated differently united states brazil .
portuguese plantation owners also kept female amerindian african slaves `` concubines '' .
offspring often assigned intermediate status white master-father brown/black slave-mother plantation supervisors domestic servants big house .
slowly `` miscegenation '' produced sub-populations mestizos mulattos .
mestizos mulattos intermarried .
gradually wide spectrum `` colours '' white shades brown shades grey black evolved brazilians thus appear .

corporate social responsibility caroline yeoh introduction idea business one one objective - maximize profits - familiar position taken business managers long time .
contemporary proponent classical view widely respected nobel prize-winning economist milton friedman .
words friedman : ... one one social responsibility business - use resources engage activities designed increase profits long stays within rules game ... years , however , pronounced shifts views business managers `` social responsibility business '' .

changes fertility policy singapore saw swee-hock introduction term `` population policies '' usually used refer policies adopted government influence course population trends patterns country .
( l ) examples explicit population policies immigration policy regulating inflow people country , mortality policy affecting general health people , population distribution redistribution policy governing movement people within country , fertility policy affecting reproductive level population .
two types fertility policy - designed encourage childbearing designed discourage childbearing .
policies adopted governments persuade people produce fewer children order lower rate population growth known anti-natalist policies , meant opposite known pro-natalist policies .
genesis national fertility policy singapore traced election manifesto people 's action party ( pap ) prepared first general election held may .
according manifesto , family planning arrangements force totally inadequate task spreading knowledge among masses .
pap intended expand family planning organization considerably would effectively reach people island .
( ) family planning association ( fpa ) , earlier urged colonial government play direct role providing family planning services , repeated call newly elected pap government take clinics .
government , faced problems greater priority , refused , played active part promotion family planning .

final phase development national fertility policy occurred march government appointed three-man review committee look question taking fpa 's clinics .
( ) committee 's report , submitted june , recommended government takeover except three clinics .
led tabling white paper family planning parliament september , accepting recommendation committee also proposing introduction national population policy .
( ) december singapore family planning population board act , enacted , according singapore family planning population board ( sfppb ) duly established june .
( ) sfppb responsible providing family planning services general public may closed ; since services provided ministry health maternal child health centres .

granite associated igneous rocks post-jurassic period found central northern , also northeastern parts island .

seroepidemiology cytomegalovirus infection introduction cytomegalovirus ( cmv ) infection usually asymptomatic .
however , present wide spectrum disease manifestations including severe congenital malformation , mononucleosis syndrome adolescents young adults , fatal disseminated infection immunocompromised persons .
following primary infection , virus becomes latent may reactivate cause recurrent infection , especially bone marrow kidney transplant recipients .
virus excreted urine , saliva , breast milk , cervical secretions faeces .
transmission occurs vertically mother child horizontally intimate contact infectious person .
viraemia may also present asymptomatic persons , primary infection follow blood transfusion .

glimpse human immunodeficiency virus type ( hiv-i ) hong saw see department biochemistry national university singapore human immunodeficiency virus type ( hiv ) etiologically identified primary cause acquired immunodeficiency syndrome ( aids ) [ , ] .
hiv-i prototypical member lentivirinae subfamily retroviruses affecting humans .
lentiviruses characteristically cause indolent infections animal hosts infections commonly involve nervous system , producing long periods clinical latency , weak humoral immune responses .
globally , world health organization estimates million people currently infected hiv-i predicted million people seropositive virus year [ ] .
hiv-i spread sexual contact , exposure infected blood blood products , perinatal transmission mother child .
clearly , aids represents international health crisis , development drug therapies effective vaccines depends substantially complete understanding pathogenic retrovirus .
hiv-i genomic structure virion retroviruses capable replication contain three genes - namely , gag , pol env [ ] .
gag env genes encode core nucleocapsid polypeptides surface-coat proteins virus , respectively , pol gene gives rise viral reverse transcriptase enzymes .
hiv-i , however , contains kilobase rna genome , three essential genes least six additional regulatory genes ( vif , vpu , vpr , tat , rev nef ) [ figure ] .
concerted action additional genes probably underlie profound pathogenicity hiv-i .
high resolution electron microscopy revealed hiv-i virion icosahedral structure containing external spikes .
spikes formed two major envelope proteins , gp gp [ figure ] .
core hiv-i contains four nucleocapsid proteins , p , p , p p .
p protein forms chief component inner leaflet nucleocapsid , whereas myristylated pl protein associated inner surface lipid bilayer .
p protein binds directly genomic rna zinc-finger structural motif together p forms nucleoid core .
life cycle hiv-i first step infectious cycle hiv-i involves attachment virion target cell .
human cd + lymphocytes monocytes major cellular targets [ ] .
hiv-i specifically infects cells cd membrane antigen represents principal , sole high-affinity cellular receptor retrovirus .
hiv-i gp l envelope protein found bind cd strikingly high affinity .
role cd viral receptor prompted considerable interest therapeutic use soluble cd protein `` receptor decoys '' block hiv-i infection [ ] .
recent studies , however , show primary viral isolates resistant soluble cd analogues .
problems encountered short biological half-life poor tissue penetration limit usefulness soluble cd antiviral agent .
besides t-cells monocytes , hiv-i may also infect types cells , including glial cells , gut epithelium bone marrow progenitors .
whether hiv-i infection diverse cell types mediated exclusively cd involves another cellular receptor remains area active investigation .
internalization reverse transcription hiv-i virion brought inside cell via virus-mediated membranefusion [ ] , process involving viral gp envelope protein .
internalization , hiv-i virion rapidly uncoated enters replicative phase life cycle .
viral replication begins generation first-strand dna copy viral rna viral reverse transcriptase .
second-strand dna synthesis proceeds viral ribonuclease h digests original rna template yielding successful therapeutic drugs used treatment hiv-i infection dideoxynucleosides , agents specifically inhibit action viral reverse transcriptase [ ] .
compounds include 'azido- ' , '-dideoxythymidine ( zidovudine , azt ) , ' 'dideoxycytidine ( ddc ) ' 'dideoxyinosine ( ddi ) .
compounds phosphorylated active triphosphate form entry cell acts either chain terminator first strand dna simple competitor normal deoxynucleoside - ' - triphosphate .
viral reverse transcriptase appears incorporate dideoxynucleoside analogues preferentially , thus explaining therapeutic effectiveness .
zidovudine shown prolong survival reduce frequency severity opportunistic infections patients aids .
viral latency upon entry susceptible cell , hiv-i may establish latent persistent form infection .
estimated every t-cell actively producing virus , nine t-cells contain latent virus .
biochemical basis apparent viral latency poorly understood may determined overall state cellular activation .
many viruses , including hiv-i , replicate resting t-cells , presumably critical host factors absent .
however , activation t-cells antigens , mitogens , cytokines ( tumor necrosis factor-alpha interleukin-l ) gene products viruses ( human t-cell leukemia virus type l , herpes simplex virus , human herpes virus type ) creates permissive environment hiv-i replication .
activating agents known induce expression host transcription factors , initiatung cell growth thus , hiv-i replication .
morphogenesis hiv-i virion assembly hiv virion proceeds via stepwise manner , involving aggregation nbonucleoprotein core cytoplasm .
gene products gag pol genes , together vital rna form core mature virion .
assembled , cores move cell surface bud plasma membrane , acquire envelope proteins , gene products env gene .
budding process , series cleavage events occur , mediated hiv-i protease .
gag polyprotein cleaved yield pl , p , p p proteins , pol gene precursor cleaved produce viral enzymes , reverse transcriptase , integrase , ribonuclease protease .
hiv-i protease long target investigators working antiviral therapeutic agents .
protease protein purified crystallised [ l ] , intrinsic three dimensional structural information used rational development specific inhibitors .
development hiv-i vaccine early studies field vaccine development hiv-i shown successful design vaccine would complicated sequence variation env protein .
hyperimmunization chimpanzees hiv-i env protein insufficient protect animals challenge even small quantities strain hiv-i [ ] .
although initial results seemed discouraging , later studies siv-i ( simian immunodeficiency virus type ) system suggest effective hiv-i vaccine may achievable goal .
inactivated whole-virus-based siv-i vaccines confer protective immunity rhesus monkeys challenged low doses live siv-i [ l ] .
however , concerns insufficient inactivation inadequate animal models safety testing restrict use whole inactivated hiv seronegative humans .
efforts date , therefore focussed subunit vaccines .
recently , protective immunity observed macaques immunised recombinant vaccinia virus expressing siv-env protein , boosted env protein produced baculovirus-infected cells [ ] .
similar combination immunization approach evaluation humans candidate aids vaccine [ ] .
remains shown , however , whether recombinant subunit vaccines protect heterologous virus challenge .
advances malarial research teo tian seng catherine ng department biochemistry national university singapore plasmodium parasite , causative agent malaria , major scourge mankind .
million people infected malaria past years million people die every year .
early seventeenth century , extracts bark cinchona tree known effective treatment disease .
th century , quinine , quinoline alkaloid , identified active antimalarial compound cinchona bark .
quinine widely used superseded chloroquine first synthesized resochin germany .
chloroquine single successful anti-malarial drug ever developed credited saving lives countless millions people .
despite enormous benefits derived use past half century , mechanism action mystery .
significant breakthrough war malaria publication recent report slater cerami ( nature ; - , ) revealed mechanism action drug .
discovery may revolutionize way future anti-malarial drugs developed .
life cycle plasmodium parasite consists sexual phase intestine mosquito asexual multiplication phase within liver parenchyma cells erythrocytes ofthe human host .
chloroquine related quinoline-containing anti-malarials effective intra-erythrocytic stages plasmodium life cycle .
rapid multiplication ofthe parasite within erythrocytes results high demand nutrients partly haemoglobin acid food vacuoles parasites .
however , digestion abundant haemoglobin without drawbacks .
breakdown haemoglobin releases large amounts haem toxic damage biological membranes inhibit variety enzymes .
human host elaborate system reticuloendothelial cells liver , spleen bone marrow degradation excretion haem .
unlike human host , parasite unable degrade excrete haem .
sequester haem food vacuoles incorporating insoluble crystalline material called haemozoin .
haemozoin polymer haem formed covalently linking central ferric ion one haem side-chain carboxylate oxygen another .

lye introduction expandable polystyrene ( eps ) , styrofoam commonly known , used widely packaging material protection goods likely damaged transportation , storage handling .
eps-moulded article cushion produced commercially means process quite similar plastic injection process , except heat required fusion eps material beads obtained injecting steam aluminium mould .
fusion , mould cooled moulded cushion ejected .
inefficient use energy , cyclic heating subsequent cooling mould , led investigations using microwave energy alternative source energy processing eps .
earlier studies carried using microwave energy unable produce desired quality cushions without inclusion carboxylmethylcellulose ( cmc ) , binding agent assist fusion beads [ ] , sodium chloride , water-soluble salt used raise boiling point water enable fusion take place [ ] .
investigations provided indication microwave energy used processing eps .
however , addition binding agent surfactant unacceptable packaging industry especially packaging sensitive electronic products agents likely contaminate corrode finished goods .
later study conducted yeong et al. , [ ] used water medium assist fusion eps beads .
study , eps cushions moulded using pre-expanded beads commercial process well raw eps beads , thereby eliminating intermediary pre-expansion stage .
various process parameters quality moulded cushion studied .
dimensional accuracy uniformity bulk density cushion found comparable attained commercial moulded products using steam-injection process .
however , studies [ ] , using custom-designed mould , two main problems faced .
firstly , fusion beads , especially close top mould , entirely satisfactory secondly , compressive strength moulded cushion completely explored comparisons made commercially-processed cushions .
present investigation , two problems addressed .
preliminary investigations made placing mould horizontal vertical planes study effect fusion moulded cushion .
subsequently , mechanism designed used rotate mould processing attempt achieve better fusion .
compressive strength moulded cushions various bulk densities investigated also .

however , programs normally run one time repeatedly , convenient simulate switching operation interactively .

furthermore , interconnected distribution network , difficult determine appropriate setting overcurrent relays [ ] ensure selected equipment operate isolate fault system affect minimum interruption consumers .
paper introduces microcomputer-based interactive distribution system simulator provides integrated tool full graphic features scada type operating environment engineers perform conveniently complete task operational planning one single process .
additional built-in automated l load flow , automated fault current calculation , integrates modelling protective relay , calculation tripping time tripping breakers followed post fault load flow calculation .

distribution system representation distribution system defined represented simulator two files named nameid.dat nameid.dw nameid name particular distribution system .
thus , different distribution system represented using different nameid .
dat file contains basic network parameters number busbars , number lines , busbar loading , line transformer impedance , line transformer connection , source impedance , etc. , also breaker open/close status , ct ratio , type relay , relay setting breaker etc .
dw file picture file used generate single line diagram distribution system .
two data files built-in fault current calculation , overcurrent relay modelling load flow , plus necessary facilities man-machine interface , distribution simulator thus constructed software microcomputer .
run-time , particular distribution system selected , simulator reads picture file nameid.dw displays single line diagram vdu .
based network parameters including breaker open/close status file nameid.dat , simulator calculates load flow displays current flow line single line-diagram .
simulator constantly monitor breaker status , feeder loading model operation protective relays .
fault applied user , simulator calculates fault current distribution operating time relays .
breaker connected relay lowest operating time trip .
whenever breaker trips hence alters network configuration , simulator determines whether fault totally isolated .
fault isolated , re-calculates fault current distribution continues modelling relays repeatedly breakers trip fault totally isolated .

khor introduction hot isostatic pressing , commonly known hipping , regarded thermomechanical technique employing , simultaneously , elevated temperature isostatic pressure process parts otherwise difficult , impossible , fabricate conventional routes [ ] .
first invented mid-fifties diffusion-bonding fuel elements nuclear industry , technique soon found many areas application , casting [ ] , powder metallurgy [ ] ceramics [ ] .
perhaps fair add major obstacles wider industrial application hip tremendously high capital operational costs .
preparation parts encapsulation poses yet another problem terms productivity cost .
improvements adaptation process equipment suggested tried experimentally .
ingenious innovation combining processes single piece equipment , expected obstacles commercial viability slowly removed .
, equipment capabilities extent higher operational temperatures pressures , research hipping advanced materials composites expected .
particular , much research effort focused areas metal matrix composites [ , ] , diffusion bonding [ ] hipping rejuvenation process creep damage [ ] .
compared uniaxial hot pressing , hip offers several advantages .
first instance , isostatic nature pressure permits processing parts geometrical complexities .
, applied pressure may exceed yield strengths metals elevated temperatures , renders process versatile .
chief advantage manufacturing perspective , however , must surely lie hip fabricate parts near-net-shape .
use advanced ceramics much felt aerospace , chemical electronic industries .
rigidity , chemical inertness thermal stability render materials suitable high technologies .
owing inherent brittleness , however , advanced ceramics typically joined metals engineering applications .
ceramic / metal bond couples found electron tubes , multi-layer substrates capacitors , metal matrix composites , etc .
whatever service environment , physical , chemical , mechanical load , thermal electrical , adhesion integrity ceramic / metal couple basic technical pre-requisite .
.
diffusion bonding ) diffusion bonding essentially micro-deformation process joins two solid surfaces using applied load elevated temperature vacuum protective atmosphere .
load applied must sufficiently low avoid macro deformation parent materials , whilst temperatures employed usually .
- .
tm , tm absolute melting temperature lesser refractory material .
processing time temperature depends much bond strength required materials involved .
typically , ranges several hours .
tm minutes .
tm .
cline [ ] proposed two-stage mechanism diffusion bonding process .
firstly , applied load causes plastic deformation surface asperities , particularly softer two mating surfaces , reduces interfacial voids .
subsequently , bond develops diffusion controlled mechanisms grain-boundary diffusion power-law creep .
today , many theoretical models designed predict dominant mechanism various stages diffusion bonding [ ] .
hot isostatic pressing diagrams also provided relate respective mechanisms processing parameters [ ] .
figure shows , schematically , sequential mechanism diffusion bonding ceramic metal proposed derby [ ] .
form effective bond , mating surfaces must come close atomic contact .
hence , original surface finishes must good free contaminants .
relatively higher hip pressures - mpa , however , surface finishes become less critical finishes .
um ra successfully hip-bonded [ ] .

ways law - maintenance issue catherine tay swee kian b ( hons . )
( london ) ; ( london ) ; lincoln 's inn , barrister-at-law ; advocate & solicitor supreme court singapore ; senior lecturer law , national university singapore married woman , whether muslim non-muslim , entitled apply women 's charter reasonable maintenance husband children .
children include illegitimate children children accepted member family .
maintenance order may made , divorce .
court may order husband pay lump sum make monthly allowance maintenance .
court take following circumstances account making maintenance order : ) financial needs wife child/children ; ) income earning capacity , , property financial resources wife child ; ) physical mental disability wife child ; ) standard living enjoyed wife husband/father neglected refused provide reasonable maintenance wife child .
make complaint complaint first made magistrate court .
complaint , wife must state husband 's earnings , number dependent children , amount maintenance need circumstances husband failed provide .
magistrate order summons sent husband day fixed court hearing .
magistrate make maintenance order husband hearing evidence .
husband always appeal thinks maintenance order unfair .
later , circumstances change , instance , husband earns , wife finds maintenance order inadequate , either party may return court variation order .
husband fails make one payments , fined imprisoned month arrears .
husband salaried job , wife ask court attachment earning order .
order husband 's employer pay amount maintenance court officer every month paying husband salary .
wife collects maintenance officer every month .
attachment order force , husband must inform court resigns job gets re-employed .
making maintenance order , court try enable wife children enjoy lifestyle family used marriage broke .
sue arrears maintenance accrued three years decided take legal action .
question bought drink shop near home .
drank little realised drink tasted bad .
poured contents glass found decomposed worms drink .
ill days .
please advise legal rights ?
answer : manufacturer responsible taking reasonable care avoid acts omissions reasonably foresee would injure neighbour .
neighours persons closely directly affected mindful acts omissions .
case , manufacturer drink owes duty care right action manufacturer negligence .
alternatively , could sue seller drink since contract sale drink seller .
ground action would breach merchantable quality sale goods act .

ways law separation agreements catherine tay swee kian b ( hons ) ( london ) ; ( london ) ; lincoln 's inn , barrister-at-law ; advocate & solicitor supreme court singapore ; senior lecturer law , national university singapore separation alternative divorce .
separation , spouses free make binding agreements regarding relationship , , live together .
number ways separating .
firstly , make simple agreement live apart .
, make formal agreement , known separation deed , live apart .
thirdly , judicial separation .
court also order spouses live apart .
simple separation agreement couple may simply decide separate .
agreement made spouses live apart contrary public policy valid enforced court .
formal separation deed formal separation deed reduces likelihood future argument exactly agreed .
typical separation deed contains provision maintenance spouse , custody children mutual covenant , one spouse releasing marital duty cohabit .
deed may also touch family assets spouses .
court may vary terms deed thinks .
woman 's point view , important separation deed entitles maintenance .
may presently need maintenance husband .
still protect inserting nominal sum maintenance , need maintenance later , ask court increase .
maintenance clause , wife may later find difficult persuade court order paid maintenance .
judicial separation decree judicial separation suitable proposition spouses unhappy marriage obtain divorce due parental pressure religious reasons .
decree judicial separation , marriage terminated even though subsists .
words , upon judicial separation , spouses longer need cohabit .
obtain judicial separation must prove facts divorce petition , adultery desertion .
judicial separation allows rights divorce .
neither spouse free remarry judicial separation end marriage way divorce .
judicial separation ends spouses ' obligations live together .
also prevent spouse getting divorce she/he .
indeed , judicial separation easily converted divorce .
court order court order couple live apart real danger domestic violence wife children .
court may make exclusion order whereby husband ordered matrimonial house , thus leaving wife children peace .
court may also make non-molestation protection order , ie ordering husband stop threatening assaulting wife and/or children .
question meaning 'vicarious liability ' ?
came across term magazine .
answer vicarious liability means responsibility one person torts ( ie wrongful acts ) committed another .
therefore , principal may liable torts agents .
likewise , master liable torts servants .

price mentioned agreement .
bound sell computer ?
please advise .
answer contract valid binding even price mentioned , show negotiations still going .

reasonable price depends circumstances case .
words , parties silent price entered valid contract , courts imply reasonable price .
case , friend pay reasonable price computer .

instead , person acts without much thought , result overpowering emotion like anger frustration .

allergic contact dermatitis clinical condition occurs following repeated contact plant allergic phenomenon .
sensitising substance plants reacts protein skin form antigen .

freshwater habitats divided several categories .
freshwater biologists limnologists , recognise two basic types lentic lotic .
lentic habitats bodies flowing water .
lotic habitats stationary water bodies .
division merely one convenience often merge one another .
categories proposed late professor johnson , ie whether forest open-country , useful followed .
open-country fresh waters - usually areas developed include agricultural land large bodies water .
include : ponds , lakes reservoirs ; monsoon canals drains ; rural streams riverine habitats .
water habitats generally eutrophic ( high levels organic matter nutrients ) ; clear due suspended silt , soil particles phytoplankton ; almost neutral slightly acidic alkaline ( ph - ) ; moderately hard ( ie dissolved salts like calcium bicarbonates ) .
water raw water reservoirs generally cleaner .
forest fresh waters - ponds ; shallow streams freshwater swamps .
water forest areas generally oligotrophic ( low levels organic matter nutrients ) ; relatively clear ( unless disturbed ) ( little phytoplankton ) ; acidic highly acidic ( ph - soft ( ie almost dissolved salts ) .
freshwater environment singapore less complex neighbours owing relatively small land area , absence significantly large freshwater riverine systems .

trilobite larvae peter k l ng h yong biologists recognise term called neoteny , used describe animal retains juvenile features even grown large size sexually mature .
layman 's language , neotenic animals perhaps described `` mature overgrown babies '' !
insect world , one dramatic best known cases neoteny occurs members beetle family lycidae .
beetles ( order coleoptera ) diverse living things planet , beetle species group animals .
thus surprising beetles adopted peculiar strategy survive extremely competitive rainforest .
well known beetle larvae pupae look different adult .

also exceptionally diverse habits habitats .

females lycids hand , never seem `` grow '' .

also become large ( cm long ) , sexually mature capable reproducing !

mynas ( acridotheres spp . )

one rarest possible acquisitions must surely bright-red indo-pacific furry lobster , palinurellus wieneckii ( de man , ) .
palinurellus wieneckii small lobster standards .
adults known grow larger mm ( usually less mm ) total length , stunning bright red colour .
numerous short hairs covering body give animal common name furry lobster , although hawaii , called mole lobster ( probably shape ) .
indo-pacific furry lobster quite rare widely distributed recorded many localities throughout indo-west pacific .
today , indo-pacific furry lobster together two species ( palinurellus gundlachi western atlantic palibythis magnificus central pacific ) , placed synaxidae , family allied true spiny lobsters family palinuridae .
fact , many books , palinurellus still classified spiny lobsters palinuridae .
indo-pacific furry lobster first described araeosternus wieneckii great dutch carcinologist j g de man .
species named honour collector , mr wieneck .
de man thought species unusual different typical spiny lobsters established new genus species , araeosternus .
even established new subfamily , araeosterninae .
unfortunately , de man know german carcinologist , e von martens described similar looking animal ( named palinurellus gundlachi ) western atlantic three years earlier named new genus species , palinurellus gundlachi .
de man , learning von martens ' species year later , realised although species , araeosternus wieneckii different von martens ' palinurellus gundlachi , genus araeosternus certainly palinurellus .
rules taxonomy , genus name palinurellus priority araeosternus , de man dutifully transferred species genus palinurellus .
de man ( ) right recognising animal belonged grouping distinct typical spiny lobsters , hence establishment new subfamily , araeosterninae .
unfortunately de man , lost credit discovery - - mere four months !
march great english carcinologist , c bate , named new family , synaxidae , american lobster also described new , synaxes hybridica .
bate 's animal proved von martens ' palinurellus gundlachi .
von martens ' animal named earlier ( vs ) , species must called palinurellus gundlachi .
sometimes quirky rules zoological nomenclature mandate rules priority also apply family subfamily names .
bate recognised palinurellus separate family ( synaxidae ) march .
de man recognised palinurellus separate subfamily ( araeosterninae ) july .
since genus palinurellus today recognised distinct family group ( allied genus palibythus ) , earliest name used must applied group .
grouping subfamily rank upgraded full family status ( ie de man 's araeosterninae becomes araeosternidae ) , synaxidae araeosternidae technically denote grouping !
bate 's synaxidae earlier valid name four months !

client/servers successful ... client/server systems successful implemented part firm 's re-engineering strategy kevin lim large companies replacing older mainframes minicomputers client/server systems .
reasons many .
network pcs workstations - - client/server applications built - - costs less mainframe equivalent power .
client/server solutions also involve distributed computing work shared client ( pc ) server ( either high-end pc workstation ) .
hence database even heavy-duty engineering program produced pc , server acting backup means distributing data .

unnecessarily slows system .
switching client/server systems always positive .
international data corporation ( idc ) , research firm , following survey large users us earlier year , said : `` mainframes clearly involve higher capital costs .
however , believe mistake extrapolate higher capital costs necessarily incur higher operating costs. ''
indeed , certain complex applications , mainframes enjoy distinct cost advantages local area networks ( lans ) . ''

failures however never highlighted .

even companies prepared pay staff , may get enough professionals familiar client/server systems , said mr lionel lim , asean managing director sun microsystems .

feedback government 's green plan , society proposed `` exchange '' save declared singapore 's two `` five star '' nature areas sites .

areas add , ha , per cent singapore 's total land area - equal toa payoh estates .

guide orchids singapore dr hugh w tan & prof hew choy sin department botany national university singapore starting orchid collection starting grow orchids , would useful talk people experience orchid growing .
orchids easier grow others flower freely .
dendrobiums , example , rather robust less susceptible bacterial attacks phalaenopsises .
arandas ascocendas free-flowering .
care must taken choose plants fit home environment .
conditions hdb flats condominiums drier shadier gardens nurseries .
duration direction sunshine important considerations home orchid growing .
according many orchid growers , four basic rules observe growing orchids well : ) purchase good healthy plants. )
house adequately. )
water feed correctly. )
protect pests diseases .
growing orchids pots orchids grown orchid pots wooden baskets .
orchid pots holes bottom well sides .
wooden baskets commonly used growing strap-leaved vandas ascocendas .
special pots used cultivation phalaenopsises .
well-built , hard symmetrical charcoal and/ red brick main potting media epi phytic orchids .
broken hammer chips .
size charcoal brick chip depends size plant pot .
plants finer roots ( oncidiums dendrobiums ) grow better pieces charcoal brick ( - cm across ) provide better retention water nutrients .
however time , may result poorer aeration .
ensure better aeration , place larger pieces bricks charcoal bottom pot .
charcoal used , useful place pieces red brick top charcoal plants grown full sunlight .
charcoal absorbs light readily surface temperature charcoal may rise c noon .
red colour brick chips tends reflect away infrared rays , source heat .
( traditionally , roofing tiles reddish ) .
potted orchids require dry environment around roots watering medium must well-drained .
potting monopodial orchids ( arandas , arachnises , arantheras , holttumaras , mokaras vandas ) , cutting first placed centre pot .
plants least two aerial roots used planting .
bricks and/ charcoal chips filled around cutting , larger chips bottom progressively smaller ones top pot .
cutting heavy , use bricks anchor better .
charcoal less dense .
sympodial orchids dendrobiums oncidiums planted oldest pseudo bulb close side pot newest pseudobulb facing centre pot .
provide ample space new pseudobulb grow .
planting sympodial orchids , use plants least two backbulbs .
psuedobulbs , food reserves available clump new growth recovery faster .
generally , orchids repotted every three years roots overgrown pots .
freshly repotted plants kept away rain strong sunlight .
orchids , like plants , require sunlight manufacture food .
generally , orchids divided three major groups according light requirements - shade , ( % full sunlight ) , partial shade ( - % full sunglight ) full sunlight ( % ) arachnises arandas , arantheras holttumaras , mokaras terete semi-terete vandas grow well full sunlight ; strapped leaved vandas phalawnopsises shade-loving ; endrobriums oncidiums grow well partial shade .
watering watering little , much right amount .
orchids often killed excessive watering rather little watering .
leaves dull looking bottom leaves drying dropping , signs plants receiving little water .
excessive watering encourages fungal bacterial infection .
also , leaves become yellowish weak , showing signs starvation roots growing well .
new growth roots good sign note whether orchids growing well .
singapore , water plant least day .
fertilizers orchids , like green plants , require trace major elements .
adequate supply elements form fertilizers essential healthy growth orchid plants .
fertilizers gaviota , welgrow , hyponex nitrophoska commonly used .
fertilizers contain three major elements nitrogen ( n ) , phosphorus ( p ) potassium ( k ) different proportions trace elements .
gaviota npk ratio : : whereas gaviota ratio : : .
general , high nitrogen content favours vegetative growth large quantities potassium phosphorous encourage flowering .
good practice supply orchids lower dosage fertilizers frequent intervals .
avoid excessive accumulation nutrients potting media wastage fertilizer .
one never exceed recommended dosage .
extra fertilizers make plants grow faster may kill instead !
using chemical fertilizers , important dissolve fertilizer completely use .
, crystals may deposited leaves roots cause burn injury plant .
solution sprayed leaves roots .
common practice apply fertilizers late afternoon sun gone .
droplets fertilizers leaves act small lenses concentrate light heat sun 's rays cause burn one sprays late morning noon .
organic fertilizers may also applied plant .
usually derived chicken dung bat bird guano ( droppings ) applied rate teaspoonful per pot every months .
needless say , follow recommendations given instructions .
advantage breaking slowly providing sustained supply nutrients period time .
initially growth vigorous application .
however , organic fertilizers tend odour ultimately break close potting medium leading poor drainage root rot .
pests diseases section , provided brief descriptions .
details , refer readers following books published primary production department , ministry national development , national development building , maxwell road , singapore , republic singapore : ) chuo , s.k .

agriculture bulletin .
diseases orchids singapore orchids like plants attacked various kinds pests diseases .
attacks occur plants grown best cultural conditions .
common pests orchid cultivation include insects ( scale , mealy-bugs , yellow beetle , caterpillars , thrips ) non-insects ( red spider mite , snails slugs ) .
orchids infected bacteria , fungi viruses .
